title: Tibble versus data.frame
description: Tibbles differ from data.frames in subsetting, printing, and never coercing strings to factors.
body: Use when choosing or mixing tibbles and base data.frames. A tibble never coerces strings to factors, never partial-matches column names, and df[, 'col'] returns a one-column tibble rather than dropping to a vector as a data.frame does by default. Do not assume identical subsetting behavior between them. Actions: rely on tibble's stricter, predictable rules and use [[ ]] or pull() when you want a vector. Validation signals include the returned object's class matching what downstream code expects. Risk boundary: a function expecting a vector but handed a one-column tibble fails or misbehaves.
